definition: A structural JSX-authoring preference governing how a parent's compound children materialize: options (a flat data prop, options={[…]}) vs compound (subcomponents, <Parent.Option> authored as children). Both are faithful lowerings of the same authored HTML; the difference is the child tree's shape, which makes this axis orthogonal to the attribute-spelling JSX dialect (#235, html/react) that leaves the tree identical. The compound form round-trips rich-markup children losslessly (its children are JSX); the options form needs a render slot for the same case (see Compound-Child Lowering).
usage: A soft authoring preference, not a protocol — it offends a convention rather than breaking interop (the #150 protocol-vs-preference test) — so any enforcement lowers into an incumbent linter, never a Web Everything sheriff. The adapter is default-less: it resolves the dialect from a development-preference config the project extends in a chain (team → business line → company → a base config offered online); options is what a native-/static-first base config sets at the root, not a constant baked into the adapter. Input accepts either form regardless of the codegen choice (round-trip), mirroring how jsxToHtml ingests either attribute dialect. Alignment task: fold the adapter's baked DEFAULT_DIALECT into the base config — the same config-extends-platform-default migration tracked in #243.
